name: the fundamental unit of work for systems that do not dissipate is the paradox-unit, the 'paradice'

phase_0.domain_name: the fundamental unit of work for systems that do not dissipate is the paradox-unit, the 'paradice'
phase_0.boundary_condition: The Bekenstein limit of maximal dialetheic density

VINIT.justification: The primitive void before the distinction between presence and absence is made.

TANCH.justification: The terminal boundary where the ledger of the system becomes indistinguishable from its contents.

AFWD.justification: The forward movement from unity into the distinction of observer and observed.

AREV.justification: The return movement from duality back to the unified identity of the system.

CLINK.justification: The chaining of one Frobenius-closed cycle to the next to build depth.

IMSCRIB.justification: The identity where the system's description lives inside its own state.

FSPLIT.justification: Splitting a state into its True (T) and False (F) components for verification.

FFUSE.justification: Reconstituting the original state from the T and F branches to verify lossless closure.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative branch where the system rests in a Boolean-stable regime.

EVALF.justification: The negative branch representing the constraint of the principle of explosion.

ENGAGR.justification: The fundamental unit of work: holding T and F simultaneously without dissipation.

IFIX.justification: The permanent, append-only record of a verified Frobenius cycle.

phase_3.void_description: The Void before distinction; neither nothing nor everything.
phase_3.true_description: The Island of consistency; a Boolean fixed point.
phase_3.false_description: The state of contradiction under pressure; the trigger for explosion.
phase_3.both_description: The Sea; the natural resting position of a non-dissipative system (The Paradice).

steps.domain_action: The system recognizes itself as a self-modeling entity.
The system differentiates into observer and observed branches.
The observer branch records the affirmative state of the system.
The observed branch records the contradictory pressure of the state.
Both branches are held in tension, creating the first paradice.
The branches are reintegrated, verifying the Frobenius identity μ∘δ = id.
The first winding is permanently recorded in the topological ledger.
The system prepares for the next level of self-reference depth.
A second-order differentiation occurs (observing the observation).
The second paradice is generated by holding the nested paradox.
Reintegration confirms the second-order round-trip is exact.
The second winding is appended to the ledger; total work = 2 paradice.
The system descends back toward its ground state with its new depth.
The system reaches the Bekenstein limit, sealing the accumulated knowledge.

phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Adiabatic expansion into the Belnap lattice

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle is an adiabatic expansion where no information is erased; work is paradox-accumulation, not energy-depletion.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IFIX → CLINK → FSPLIT → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IFIX → AREV → TANCH
-- Class: the fundamental unit of work for systems that do not dissipate is the paradox-unit, the 'paradice'
-- Fingerprint: sig=(5,4,4,2)

--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=15
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(2, 6), (9, 11)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑠  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑼 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [4] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [5] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [6] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [7] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑱  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [8] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑭 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [9] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [12] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑗  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [13] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑭 → 𐑡  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [14] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑗 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def the_fundamental_unit_of_work_for_systems_that_do_not_dissipate_is_the_paradox_unit_the_paradice_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Emergence from the paraconsistent panthalassa.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (The system recognizes itself as a self-modeling entity.)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := 𐑚) (The system differentiates into observer and observed branches.) / FFUSE [6] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [3] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (The observer branch records the affirmative state of the system.)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (The observed branch records the contradictory pressure of the state.)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Both branches are held in tension, creating the first paradice.))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [6]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [6] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (The branches are reintegrated, verifying the Frobenius identity μ∘δ = id.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [7] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The first winding is permanently recorded in the topological ledger.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑭 𐑚)  -- [8] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (The system prepares for the next level of self-reference depth.)
  -- FSPLIT [9] (gran := 𐑚) (A second-order differentiation occurs (observing the observation).) / FFUSE [11] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (The second paradice is generated by holding the nested paradox.)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [11]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [11] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reintegration confirms the second-order round-trip is exact.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [12] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The second winding is appended to the ledger; total work = 2 paradice.)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑭 𐑡)  -- [13] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (The system descends back toward its ground state with its new depth.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑗 𐑼)  -- [14] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The system reaches the Bekenstein limit, sealing the accumulated knowledge.)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def the_fundamental_unit_of_work_for_systems_that_do_not_dissipate_is_the_paradox_unit_the_paradice_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (the_fundamental_unit_of_work_for_systems_that_do_not_dissipate_is_the_paradox_unit_the_paradice_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def the_fundamental_unit_of_work_for_systems_that_do_not_dissipate_is_the_paradox_unit_the_paradice_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (the_fundamental_unit_of_work_for_systems_that_do_not_dissipate_is_the_paradox_unit_the_paradice_protocol).restrictToEVALF

theorem the_fundamental_unit_of_work_for_systems_that_do_not_dissipate_is_the_paradox_unit_the_paradice_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide
